description: A celestial mitten drifts through the cosmos, the Great Lakes State transformed into a buoyant balloon formation where teal peninsulas and sapphire waters dance among the stars in eternal orbital celebration.

learnings.pattern_analysis: The distinctive mitten shape of Michigan's Lower Peninsula provides an immediately recognizable silhouette that translates naturally into balloon form. The Upper Peninsula as a secondary floating element creates visual balance and geographic accuracy.
learnings.color_notes: The teal-to-deep-green gradient (#2A9D8F) evokes both the forested landscape and balloon glossiness, while the Great Lakes blue (#457B9D) provides cool contrast. The warm glow (#F4A261) adds celebration warmth against the deep space background (#0D1B2A).
learnings.animation_insights: Multiple float animations with staggered delays create organic balloon movement without synchronization. The orbital animation for accent elements adds cosmic scale, while twinkling stars at varied speeds generate living space atmosphere.
learnings.composition_principles: Approximately 40% negative space maintained on left and right edges emphasizes the vast emptiness of space. The Michigan formation occupies the left-center while celebration balloons balance on the right, creating asymmetric but weighted composition.
